{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[burial chamber]] made of cut [[tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Asuka Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Marukoyama Kofun]][[en:Marukoyama Kofun]][[ja:マルコ山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11341265]] Wikidata name: Marukoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460889|N|135.790611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of cut [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenchi|Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of cut [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenchi|Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

[[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of cut [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenchi|Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yoshino Line|Yoshino Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Marukoyama Kofun]][[en:Marukoyama Kofun]][[ja:マルコ山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Marukoyama Kofun]][[en:Marukoyama Kofun]][[ja:マルコ山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Marukoyama Kofun]][[en:Marukoyama Kofun]][[ja:マルコ山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11341265]] Wikidata name: Marukoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460889|N|135.790611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka}}, [[Nara prefecture]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenchi|Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Asuka Station|en|Asuka Station|WD=Q4811860|lt=Asuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Yoshino Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Marukoyama Kofun | native_name =マルコ山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Marukoyamakofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marukoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460889|N|135.790611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka}}, [[Nara prefecture]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenchi|Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Asuka Station|en|Asuka Station|WD=Q4811860|lt=Asuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Yoshino Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Marukoyama Kofun'''|マルコ山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mayumi neighborhood of the village of {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka, Nara}}, [[Nara prefecture]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2038|title=マルコ山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marukoyama Kofun is located in the southern end of the Mayumi Hills, which spread across the western part of Asuka Village, and is surrounded by other tombs from the end of the Kofun period, such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and [[Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus was built on a flattened out area on the southern slope of a ridge running east-to-west, and has a diameter of 15 meters and height of 5.3 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1977 and 1978, revealing that the tumulus was built in two tiers, and although previously regarded as an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, was in fact a rare hexagonal {{nihongo|''rokkaku-fun''|六角墳|}}. Furthermore, the tumulus contained a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stones orientated to the south. The interior walls, including the floor, are covered with plaster, which has a similar structure to the Takamatsuzuka Kofun and [[Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]]. The ceiling is carved into a roof shape, which is also similar to the Kitora Kofun; however, no murals were found. Excavated artifacts include fragments of a lacquered wooden coffin, copper nails, iron nails, and gilt bronze coffin ornaments, as well as a gilt bronze sword fitting as a {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. Human bones of a man believed to be in his 30s were also found, The identity of the buried person is unknown, but it is almost certain that it was a member of the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], possibly a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenchi|Emperor Tenchi]]. Based on the shape of the burial chamber and the excavated remains, the tumulus is thought to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Asuka Station|en|Asuka Station|WD=Q4811860|lt=Asuka Station|1=Asuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Yoshino Line|1=Yoshino Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.asukamura.jp/gyosei_bunkazai_shitei_11_maruko1.html Asuka village home page]{{in lang|ja}}
